findings.reasoning: The rule triggers only if the page is advertising a deposit product (e.g., a checking/savings account requiring FDIC insurance disclosure). This page is a tax-tips article about SBA 7(a) small business loans, tax implications of loans, and alternatives like equity, lines of credit, and tax credits. It does mention Credit Karma Money checking accounts and Router Accounts in the boilerplate footer disclosures, referencing that 'Banking services for Credit Karma Money accounts are provided by MVB Bank, Inc., Member FDIC' and that Router Accounts are at an 'FDIC insured bank' - but these are incidental mentions of banking partners for refund-delivery/loan-repayment mechanisms, not the product being advertised on this page. The page's advertised products are TurboTax tax-prep offerings and business loan alternatives; no deposit account product (e.g., Credit Karma Money Spend account) is being marketed as the featured product here, and no specific FDIC insurance amount language is present or required to be present as the core message. Since no deposit product is the subject of advertisement on this page, the trigger is not met.

The rule applies only when a deposit product is being advertised and therefore FDIC insurance language would be required. This page discusses SBA loans, PPP/EIDL loans, tax tips, TurboTax software, Credit Karma Money accounts, and various loan programs like TurboTax Refund Advance and File Now, Pay Later. While Credit Karma Money accounts and a Router Account with an FDIC-insured bank are mentioned, these are referenced in the context of money movement and loan disbursement, not as advertised deposit products on this page. There is no dedicated promotion of a bank deposit account as the product being advertised, nor any FDIC insurance language that would fall under this rule. Consequently, the trigger condition for R-03 is not met, making the rule not applicable.

findings.evidence_quote: * Get your tax refund up to 5 days early in a Credit Karma Money™ Account: When it’s time to file, have your tax refund direct deposited to a Credit Karma Money™ checking account, and you could receive your funds up to 5 days early. If you choose to pay your tax preparation fee with TurboTax using your federal tax refund or if you choose to take the TurboTax Refund Advance loan, you will not be eligible to receive your refund up to 5 days early with Credit Karma. 5-day early program may change or discontinue at any time. Up to 5 days early access to your federal tax refund is compared to standard tax refund electronic deposit and is dependent on and subject to IRS submitting refund information to the bank before release date. IRS may not submit refund information early. Excludes business tax returns. Credit Karma is not a bank. Banking services for Credit Karma Money accounts are provided by MVB Bank, Inc., Member FDIC. Maximum balance and transfer limits apply per account. For more information, please visit <https://turbotax.intuit.com/credit-karma-money/>.
findings.location: TurboTax Online/MOBILE disclosures — Credit Karma Money bullet
findings.reasoning: Trigger: The page promotes a specific deposit product (Credit Karma Money checking account) as part of an offer to get a refund up to 5 days early, so the rule applies. Requirement: The FDIC insurance statement must say the deposit product is FDIC-insured up to $250,000 through [Bank]. The page only states “Member FDIC” and names MVB Bank, Inc., without the $250,000 coverage limit or the required construction, so Axis A is not met. Axis B: The wording does not match the approved library entry. Strongest counter-reading: One might argue this is merely informational, not an advertisement, or that “Member FDIC” is sufficient. However, the bullet actively encourages using the Credit Karma Money checking account to receive funds early, which is promotional, and “Member FDIC” alone does not satisfy the specified language or coverage limit requirement.
